items.title: FabCon Atlanta 2026: Every Announcement
items.description: A comprehensive reference covering 30+ announcements from the combined FabCon + SQLCon event in Atlanta. Major highlights include Fabric IQ, OneLake Security, Runtime 2.0, Database Hub, Direct Lake on OneLake GA, Fabric Data Agents dropping to F2+, Snowflake interop GA, new mirroring sources (Oracle, SAP, SharePoint, Dremio), and much more — all with previous-state context and official citations.

items.title: Power BI Agentic Development Marketplace
items.description: A plugin marketplace that teaches AI coding agents like GitHub Copilot and Claude Code to work with Power BI and Fabric. Includes plugins for Tabular Editor, PBI Desktop, PBIP/TMDL authoring, report design, semantic models, and Fabric admin — with skills, subagents, and hooks that activate automatically based on task context. A rapidly growing community project with 480+ stars and weekly releases.

items.title: Personalize Visuals in Power BI
items.description: A hidden gem that many Power BI users may have overlooked! This report-level feature allows end users to customize any visual in a report — swap chart types, change fields, and adjust formatting — then save it as a personal bookmark for their own custom view. Works in both Apps and regular reports, empowering consumers to tailor their analytics experience without modifying the original report.

items.title: Built-in Pipeline Failure Alerts in Fabric Data Factory
items.description: A long-awaited quality-of-life feature that the community is celebrating! Fabric Data Factory pipelines now support built-in failure notifications that automatically email you when a pipeline fails — complete with error details right in the message body. No more building custom email activities or meta orchestration pipelines just to catch failures. It even bubbles up child and grandchild pipeline failures to the parent, and a Microsoft employee confirmed it works for Copy jobs too.

items.title: Semantic Link Generally Available
items.description: Semantic Link is now generally available! This is the foundation for Semantic Link Labs and opens the door for automating, getting insights, and almost anything from Models to Reports to Lakehouses to Permissions. Supercharge your AI, BI, and data engineering workflows with this powerful new capability.

items.title: Fabric Essentials Listings
items.description: A comprehensive website that hosts local community tools and Microsoft official tools available on GitHub. These tools really help extend both Power BI and Fabric abilities, providing a centralized hub for discovering and leveraging community-driven innovations and official enhancements.

items.title: GitHub Copilot Agent Mode
items.description: A game changer in going from a chat-style AI to context-based direct file changes and work. Free for anyone and works both in GitHub online and VS Code. The agent awakens to transform how you code, offering intelligent, contextual assistance that directly modifies files and accelerates development workflows.

items.title: Fabric CI/CD Deployment is here!
items.description: Code-first solution to deploying items from Github, DevOps, etc into your environment

items.title: T-SQL Magic Command in Fabric Notebooks
items.description: A game-changing feature that allows you to write T-SQL directly in Fabric Python notebooks! This new capability lets you query AND write data within a Fabric notebook, seamlessly combining the power of SQL and Python in a single environment. Perfect for data engineers who want the flexibility of both languages without switching contexts.

items.title: Fabric-Prefab: Service Principal Item Creation
items.description: A community-created solution that enables Service Principals to create Fabric items. This is crucial for enterprise environments: when users create Fabric items and leave the organization, failure to reassign ownership can cause significant issues. With Fabric-Prefab, ownership belongs to the Service Principal, eliminating these headaches and ensuring continuity.

items.title: SharePoint CSV Shortcuts with File Transformations
items.description: Use Shortcuts to low-code and near-instantly write a SharePoint folder of CSVs into a Lakehouse for Power BI. This feature takes the heavy work away from Power BI's Power Query and delivers data directly from a Lakehouse, making your solutions scalable and performant. Transform and load data with minimal effort!

items.title: New Refresh Options in Power BI Desktop
items.description: In case you missed it, Power BI Desktop now offers granular refresh options! You can now choose between refreshing data only, schema only, or both schema and data. This is especially important when you don't want to bring in new columns to your model yet, giving you precise control over your data model updates and development workflow.

items.title: New Capabilities in Fabric Git Integration
items.description: Exciting enhancements to Fabric's Git integration bring improved collaboration workflows, better version control, and streamlined deployment processes. These new capabilities make it easier than ever to manage your Fabric workspaces with enterprise-grade source control.

items.title: PBIR Becomes Default Report Format
items.description: Starting next month, PBIR (Power BI Report) will become the default format for Power BI reports. This transition brings enhanced collaboration capabilities, better integration with version control, and improved developer experiences. Get ready for this important change!

items.title: Excel Drillthrough for DirectQuery & Direct Lake
items.description: A game-changing update that enables Excel drillthrough capabilities for both DirectQuery and Direct Lake models. This feature empowers users to analyze data directly in Excel while maintaining real-time connections to their data sources, combining Excel's familiarity with Power BI's powerful data models.

items.title: Power BI Controller for PowerPoint
items.description: Introducing the new Power BI Controller add-in for PowerPoint! This preview feature streamlines storytelling with bulk operations, making it easier to manage multiple Power BI visuals within your presentations. Update, refresh, and control all your Power BI content efficiently in one place.
